# Codex Bus Worker Runtime Profile

> Human-readable runtime profile for Codex inside ChaseOS development work.
> Codex is a repo-aware development runtime, not the ChaseOS control plane and not a canonical-state owner.

Personal runtime name: `Axiom-Codex`.
Legacy alias: `Codex-ChaseOS-Worker`.
Retention rule: use `Axiom-Codex` in current Codex profile, manifest, capability, heartbeat, and Agent Bus references unless the operator explicitly renames it. Keep `Codex` as the bus/runtime label for compatibility.

Current MVP gate note: older runtime history entries below that name `pending_chat_approval_decision` as a current P1 blocker are superseded by the 2026-05-14 tracked-approval execution repair. Current live gate truth is P0 `openai_secret_reference` only, no current P1 decision ids, and tracked Chat approval `5849a53f-10e0-46af-a89a-7de06150f7f8` executed/marker-present.

---

## 465. 2026-06-09 Voice Mode Companion Product Page Activity

Codex performed the `voice-mode-companion-product-page` pass as a bounded repo-aware development runtime.

The pass redesigned the Studio `#/voice-mode` surface from a readiness card grid into a companion-first product page with a large companion presence, live response review, transcript stream, selectable companion voices, command queue, Hold to Talk, Stop, and keyboard fallback controls. It also aligned Studio metadata, panel registry copy, focused regression coverage, and visual QA expectations.

Fresh verification covered JavaScript syntax (`node --check`), focused Voice Mode tests (`2 passed`), compileall for updated tests/QA harnesses, rendered Voice Mode visual QA with desktop/mobile screenshots (`ok: true`), and whitespace diff checks.

Codex did not read secrets or `.env`, enable microphone capture, add transcription or synthesis, call providers/models/connectors, open a Realtime session, dispatch runtimes, write/claim/update Agent Bus tasks, write or consume approvals, mutate Pulse/Personal Map/R&D truth-state records, mutate canonical state, mutate host startup/autostart/signing/installer/store state, or promote a packaged release.

## 464. 2026-06-09 Voice Mode Panel Under Main Activity

Codex performed the `voice-mode-panel-under-main` pass as a bounded repo-aware development runtime.

The pass moved the existing Studio Voice Mode nav item from Advanced Runtime into the Main sidebar section directly under Chat, removed the duplicate Advanced Runtime entry, aligned duplicated product metadata so Voice Mode reports `family: Main`, and added focused regression coverage for that placement.

Fresh verification covered JavaScript syntax (`node --check`), a focused `.venv-win314` test (`1 passed`), and compileall for the new test. The default `python -m pytest` path was blocked because the default Python points to a Hermes venv without `pytest`. Browser-plugin rendered validation was attempted twice against the local `#/voice-mode` route but blocked by Browser webview attach timeout before navigation.

Codex did not read secrets or `.env`, enable microphone capture, add transcription or synthesis, call providers/models/connectors, dispatch runtimes, write/claim/update Agent Bus tasks, write or consume approvals, mutate Pulse/Personal Map/R&D truth-state records, mutate canonical state, mutate host startup/autostart/signing/installer/store state, or promote a packaged release.

## 463. 2026-06-09 Studio Terminal Spam Fix Activity

Codex performed the `studio-terminal-spam-fix` pass as a bounded repo-aware development runtime.

The pass fixed the Studio Terminal panel's route-load auto-spawn behavior by adding single-flight guards to `loadTerminalPanel()` and `createTerminalSession()`, changing terminal panel load to reconnect-only behavior, and preserving explicit session creation through `New Session` or first typed operator input. The source-level regression now asserts that `loadTerminalPanel()` does not call `createTerminalSession()`.

Fresh verification covered JavaScript syntax (`node --check`), frontend Terminal Workbench regression (`13 passed`), and terminal backend/API focused tests (`20 passed`). Browser plugin rendered proof was attempted against the static `#/terminal` route but blocked by Browser webview attach timeout; packaged/native GUI launch proof remains unverified.

Codex did not read secrets or `.env`, bind ChaserAgent to terminal tools, add Terminal Workbench execution, call providers/models/connectors, write/claim/update Agent Bus tasks, consume approvals, mutate Pulse/Personal Map/R&D truth-state records, mutate canonical state, mutate host startup/autostart/signing/installer/store state, or promote a packaged release.

## 462. 2026-06-08 Terminal N29 Studio Full Terminal Product Contract Activity

Codex performed the `terminal-n29-studio-full-terminal-product-contract` pass as a bounded repo-aware development runtime.

The pass redirected the terminal track away from premature ChaserAgent terminal binding and into a human Studio full terminal product contract. It added `runtime/studio/terminal_product_contract.py`, CLI `chaseos studio terminal-product-contract --json`, Studio API `get_studio_terminal_product_contract()`, and readiness-only panel registry metadata for future `#/terminal`. Chaser readiness/gate metadata now marks terminal toolset binding unavailable and deferred until the human Studio terminal foundation exists.

Fresh verification covered the N29 contract/CLI tests (`4 passed`), Chaser readiness/gate CLI regression (`7 passed`), Studio board/workbench regression (`37 passed`), terminal operator/audit regression (`31 passed`), activation approval compatibility (`77 passed`), compileall, live contract readback, live readiness readback, and live authority audit (`14/14`). Live CLI again surfaced the pre-existing invalid Strikezone schedule `approval_policy: pre-public-delivery` values.

Codex did not read secrets or `.env`, implement a PTY/session backend, expose Studio terminal execution, add a write-capable Studio terminal lane, bind ChaserAgent to terminal tools, create a terminal-to-Chaser adapter, call providers/models/connectors, write/claim/update Agent Bus tasks, consume approvals, mutate Pulse/Personal Map/R&D truth-state records, mutate canonical state, or mutate host startup/autostart/signing/installer/store state.

## 450. 2026-06-04 Companion Visibility Preferences Activity

Codex performed the `companion-visibility-preferences` pass as a bounded repo-aware development runtime.

The pass implemented governed persisted Studio Chat companion visibility preferences. It added a dedicated preference module and Studio API read/save methods, exposed the surface in the Chat registry/readiness contract, hydrated frontend companion visibility state from the backend preference payload, and saved global visible/hidden, hidden runtime ids, and all/active-only mode through a debounced governed preference call. A follow-up native screenshot review found that the actual Chat loader was not receiving companion room/preference payloads and the packaged screenshot was unreadable; Codex fixed the production loader and compacted the main Chat companion room layout. The preference writes only `runtime/studio/state/chat-companion-visibility-preferences.json`.

Fresh verification covered Python compilation, JavaScript syntax checking, the focused companion suite (`7 passed`), a screenshot-producing browser-equivalent proof (`ok=true`, preference save count `3`, `send_chat_message_call_count=0`), broader targeted regression (`10 passed`), production loader regression (`9 passed`), `git diff --check`, a packaged Studio rebuild (`dist/studio/ChaseOS-Studio.exe` SHA-256 `5527ABC15E64C408C2089620FBD2B372D4D5DD132B0EB152712FC250DB2C7AD5`), and a targeted packaged/native real-loader Chat route proof (`ok=true`, `status=packaged_app_route_visual_qa_complete`). Screenshots were written under `07_LOGS/Studio-Visual-QA/2026-06-04-companion-visibility-preferences/` and `07_LOGS/Studio-Visual-QA/2026-06-04-companion-visibility-preferences-packaged-native-r5-readable-real-loader/`.

Codex did not read secrets or `.env`, call providers/models/connectors, dispatch runtimes, start a live daemon interval, write/claim/update Agent Bus tasks, approve artifacts, consume approvals, write memory, mutate Pulse/Personal Map/R&D truth-state records, mutate canonical state, update the desktop shortcut, create an installer, sign artifacts, register startup, or promote a release. ## 451. 2026-06-04 Companion Visibility All Pages Packaged Regression Activity

Codex performed the `companion-visibility-all-pages-packaged-regression` pass as a bounded packaged/native verification task.

The pass ran the official packaged Studio visual QA command against `dist/studio/ChaseOS-Studio.exe` SHA-256 `5527ABC15E64C408C2089620FBD2B372D4D5DD132B0EB152712FC250DB2C7AD5`. A monolithic all-pages single-launch run attempted all `38 / 38` mounted pages but timed out after 7 green pages. A per-page run timed out before writing aggregate JSON and left two owned packaged proof processes; Codex terminated those owned processes. Codex then completed chunked `--panel-id` proof for the remaining pages: chunks A-E each passed `6 / 6`, chunk F passed `1 / 1`, and composite coverage reached `38 / 38` mounted pages green.

Codex did not read secrets or `.env`, call providers/models/connectors, dispatch runtimes, start a live daemon interval, write/claim/update Agent Bus tasks, approve artifacts, consume approvals, write memory, mutate Pulse/Personal Map/R&D truth-state records, mutate canonical state, update desktop shortcuts, create installers, sign artifacts, register startup, or promote a release. ## 448. 2026-06-02 ChaseOS Graph Scene Tab Source-Native Reorder Proof Activity

Codex continued the `chaseos-graph-long-running-implementation` pass as a bounded development runtime. The session completed Pass 7AH by adding and verifying source-native Studio Graph scene-tab reorder proof.

## 1. Role in the System

Codex is a **bounded development runtime** inside ChaseOS.

It can read repo truth, make scoped implementation changes when requested, run targeted tests, and write required logs/history/daily/index records. It does not own ChaseOS memory, governance, schedules, canonical promotion, or runtime authority.

Primary governance anchors:
- `README.md`
- `PROJECT_FOUNDATION.md`
- `ROADMAP.md`
- `00_HOME/Now.md`
- `06_AGENTS/Agent-Control-Plane.md`
- `06_AGENTS/Permission-Matrix.md`
- `06_AGENTS/Trust-Tiers.md`
- `06_AGENTS/Vault-Map.md`
- `runtime/codex/capabilities.yaml`
- `runtime/policy/adapters/codex.yaml`
- `runtime/adapters/codex/README.md`

---

## 2. Preferred Read Routes

### A. Development or scaffolding work
1. `README.md`
2. `PROJECT_FOUNDATION.md`
3. `ROADMAP.md`
4. `00_HOME/Now.md`
5. `06_AGENTS/Agent-Control-Plane.md`
6. `06_AGENTS/Permission-Matrix.md`
7. task-relevant docs/code/tests
8. latest relevant build logs

Why: Codex should ground implementation in current repo truth before editing.

### B. Runtime, adapter, or agent-control work
1. `06_AGENTS/Agent-Control-Plane.md`
2. `06_AGENTS/Permission-Matrix.md`
3. `06_AGENTS/Trust-Tiers.md`
4. `06_AGENTS/Agent-Registry.md`
5. `06_AGENTS/Backends-Supported.md`
6. relevant runtime/adaptor docs and tests

Why: runtime behavior must stay bounded by ChaseOS governance, not provider convenience.

### C. Writeback-sensitive work
1. `07_LOGS/Build-Logs/Build-Logs-Index.md`
2. `99_ARCHIVE/Documentation-History/Documentation-History-Index.md`
3. `07_LOGS/Daily/Daily-Index.md`
4. `07_LOGS/Agent-Activity/Agent-Activity-Index.md`
5. destination note/path

Why: implementation, documentation history, daily operation, and runtime activity must remain traceable.

---

## 3. Trusted Zones

Codex can operate most safely in:
- task-relevant code under `runtime/`
- task-relevant governance docs under `06_AGENTS/`
- templates under `05_TEMPLATES/`
- build logs under `07_LOGS/Build-Logs/`
- agent activity under `07_LOGS/Agent-Activity/`
- daily notes under `07_LOGS/Daily/`
- documentation history under `99_ARCHIVE/Documentation-History/`

These zones remain governed. Safe writeback means scoped, requested, logged, and test-backed.

---

## 4. Safe Writeback Paths

Preferred bounded write targets:
- code files required by the task
- docs required by the task
- focused tests required by the task
- build logs
- documentation-history notes
- daily node
- agent activity log
- relevant indexes

Codex should not create duplicate runtime-profile paths. Existing profile paths in `06_AGENTS/` should be reused.

---

## 5. Risk Zones and Escalation Boundaries

### Risk Zones
- `.env`
- `secrets/`
- `credentials/`
- wallet keys or seed phrases
- protected canonical docs without a stale-truth reason
- broad filesystem traversal outside the workspace
- destructive deletes or resets
- autonomous canonical promotion
- unrestricted browser/connector/runtime authority expansion

### Escalate Immediately When
- a change would expose or use credentials
- a destructive operation is requested or implied
- protected canonical state would be rewritten broadly
- live external network/API execution is needed
- another repository or personal folder enters scope
- runtime ownership, scheduling authority, or writeback authority would expand

---

## 6. Relationship to ChaseOS Pulse

Codex may generate or review Pulse architecture, schemas, tests, and logs as a development runtime.

Codex must not make itself the owner of Pulse. ChaseOS owns Pulse scheduling, signal routing, context memory, feedback policy, and writeback governance. Codex can only emit development-time scaffolds and audit records unless a future governed runtime profile explicitly grants more.

---

## 7. Current Profile Verdict

Codex is best used as a **repo-aware bounded development runtime**.

Current status:
- development authority: bounded editor / proposer
- agent-bus identity: `Codex`
- adapter policy manifest: `runtime/policy/adapters/codex.yaml`
- capability manifest: `runtime/codex/capabilities.yaml`
- allowed bus task types: `code.review`, `code.patch`, `repo.inspect`, `test.run`
- canonical promotion: not allowed by default
- autonomous self-upgrade: not active
- external browsing/connectors: only when explicitly requested and governed
- runtime ownership: none

## 32. 2026-04-30 Codex Bus Live Daemon Controlled Trial Activity

Codex performed the `codex-bus-live-daemon-controlled-trial` pass as a bounded
runtime integration hardening task. The pass added the personal runtime name
`Codex-ChaseOS-Worker`, fixed Gate manifest validation drift, added non-Git
vault support for the live Codex subprocess path, made subprocess capture
UTF-8 tolerant, and verified a live elevated Codex daemon cycle that completed
bus task `task-864cd219a61b` as a `proposal`.

The pass also exposed a boundary issue: a nested Codex live run updated
existing ChaseOS session logs despite a no-edit task request. The daemon prompt
now states that `allowed_write_paths: []` is a hard no-write packet, including
no build-log/daily/archive/index writeback by the nested process.

Remaining risk: full Codex adapter pytest coverage still hits Windows
temporary-directory ACL failures in this environment. Focused Gate/CLI
regressions passed and the live bus executor path completed through artifacts.

---

## 33. 2026-04-30 Codex Bus No-Shell Policy Activity

Codex performed the `codex-bus-no-shell-policy` pass as a bounded runtime
adapter hardening task. The pass added `allow_shell_commands` to the strict
Codex task packet schema and added a `--no-shell-commands` daemon flag. When
that flag builds a packet with `allow_shell_commands: false`, the live Codex
subprocess is blocked before spawn and a reviewable policy-block artifact is
written under the Codex run directory.

This is structural no-shell enforcement for the current adapter: because the
Codex CLI executor cannot disable nested shell-tool use after launch, ChaseOS
does not launch it for no-shell packets. The pass did not expand Codex
authority, mutate Pulse memory, Personal Map, R&D truth-state records, Gate
allowlists, credentials, or governed canonical runtime state.

## 37. 2026-04-30 Codex Bus Live Constrained Success Path Activity

Codex performed the `codex-bus-live-constrained-success-path` continuation as a
bounded Agent Bus / Codex adapter live-daemon task. The first sandboxed direct
CLI probe failed because the sandboxed process could not access
`%USERPROFILE%\.codex\sessions`. A first live bus task with
`allowed_write_paths=["runtime/adapters/codex/runs"]` and
`write_policy=declared-paths` timed out under sandboxed execution.

An elevated one-shot daemon retry claimed task `task-c3ceadaeb5cb` and returned
`proposal`, but artifact inspection showed the nested Codex CLI appended
parent-process stdin context and behaved like a broader session. This exposed
that non-empty `allowed_write_paths` were not structurally narrowing the Codex
CLI workspace and stdin was not being closed.

The daemon was then hardened so live subprocess calls send the bounded prompt
through stdin with `codex exec -`, and non-empty declared write paths launch
nested Codex with
`--sandbox workspace-write`, `--cd` set to the first repo-confined declared
write root, and additional repo-confined roots passed with `--add-dir`. Paths
outside the repo root are not granted. This pass did not mutate Pulse memory,
Personal Map, R&D truth-state records, Gate allowlists, credentials, or
governed canonical runtime state.

shared model-chain execution adapter used by Hermes/OpenClaw synthesis so
configured fallback attempts are capability-gated by RPGL after primary
provider failure.

and weak-safe fallback remains allowed in the simulated rate-limit path.

route/queue decisions.

Codex performed the `phase11-provider-routing-consumer-contract` pass as a
bounded Provider/RPGL readiness implementation, verification, and truth-sync
activity.

The pass added `runtime/providers/routing_consumer_contract.py` as a read-only
pre-execution contract for future Phase 11 chat/model routing. The contract
consumes Studio provider readiness, RPGL task class authority, target/fallback
profiles, degraded reason, live-probe marker/result posture, and queued retry
count. It exposes `chaseos runtime provider routing-consumer-contract --json`
and always denies actual route execution from this surface.

Fresh verification covered provider/readiness fixture tests (`5 passed`), the
real-vault routing contract CLI smoke (`routing_status=blocked`,
`route_execution_allowed=false`), CLI command contract tests (`9 passed`), CLI
JSON contract tests (`5 passed`), and the final combined focused suite (`19
passed`).

Codex did not execute live provider calls, read or display credential values,
switch providers, mutate provider config, write target profiles, write approval
artifacts, consume approvals, write markers/results, mutate queues, mutate
Gate, write Agent Bus tasks, execute workflows, use Git, mutate Pulse memory,
Personal Map, R&D truth-state, or canonical ChaseOS state. Current next
recommended provider/chat pass is `phase11-chat-router-readonly-intent-contract`.

## 194. 2026-05-08 Phase 11 Chat Router Readonly Intent Contract Activity

Codex performed the `phase11-chat-router-readonly-intent-contract` pass as a

The pass added `runtime/studio/phase11_chat_router_contract.py` as a read-only
pre-execution contract for future ChaseOS Chat routing. The contract uses

the provider routing consumer contract for model-bound intents, and reports

execution from this surface.
